Title: Fiora Laurent
Age: Current26 – 31 years old Timeline Born966 AN – 971 AN Edit

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent: Fiora was born to House Laurent of Demacia, and as their youngest daughter, she seemed destined to be nothing more than a political pawn for her house. Irritated by this, the young Fiora made a concerted effort to defy her family at every turn. Though her parents had given her dolls, Fiora studied the blade, taking her eldest brother's rapier and forcing him to give her lessons in secret. The mannequins which were meant for her gowns were instead used as practice dummies, and she became an excellent swordswoman.

children.title: Crownguard-Laurent Feud
children.textContent:
After Fiora's eighteenth birthday, her parents arranged her to be married to an outlying branch of the Crownguard family, which would form a beneficial alliance between their two houses. The summer wedding was to be held in the Great City, and King Jarvan III himself was scheduled to attend. But when the day arrived, Fiora stood up and declared that she would rather die than marry someone against her will. The outburst publicly shamed her betrothed, and his family demanded the matter be settled in a traditional duel to the death, which Fiora immediately agreed to.

Distressed that his daughter might be killed, Fiora's father Sebastien pleaded for King Jarvan's intervention. But since Fiora had already accepted, the duel could no longer be cancelled. With no other option, Sebastien invoked his right to fight in his daughter's place. The High Marshal Tianna Crownguard similarly nominated a champion to fight in her kinsman's place: this one a veteran warrior from the Dauntless Vanguard. In the face of almost certain defeat, which would lead to Fiora's exile and his house's ruin, Sebastien Laurent attempted to drug his opponent before the duel. He was caught, and arrested for violating Demacia's most fundamental code of honor.

On the eve of Sebastien's execution, Fiora visited him in his cell. The exact details of their conversation remain a secret only to her, but the next day, she approached the King with a proposition: instead of a hanging as originally planned, she would personally fight her father in a duel to the death for leadership of House Laurent. The King gave his blessing and watched from the dais as Fiora and Sebastien fought in a swift dance of blades. Both father and daughter were exceptional duelists, but in the end, she drove her rapier through his heart and bid him a tearful goodbye. After the duel, King Jarvan solemnly declared Sebastien's crimes to have been paid for in full, the feud between houses to be resolved, and Fiora the new leader of House Laurent.

children.title: Dragon at Ehrenmount
children.textContent: While at a Demacian training arena in Ehrenmount, Fiora and Garen caught two young children, Brit and Aron, playing with the equipment and pretending to be true heroes of Demacia. These children idolized Fiora and Garen respectively, but had difficulty imitating their moves. Garen jokingly asked Fiora if they should consider them for the Dauntless Vanguard. She declared that seeking glory was futile without first finding excellence, or else they might suffer a violent death. Garen believed this was harsh and started to scold her, but before they could continue the conversation, a large dragon was spotted by the guards and they went to defend the city.

children.title: Duel with Jax
children.textContent: Jax, who was looking for the finest warriors on Runeterra to join his fight against the Void, stood guarding a bridge in Demacia, snacking on eggs and blocking any soldiers who wanted to pass. He defeated fifteen elite soldiers, then another, wondering if there was no one in the kingdom who could challenge him. Fiora arrived as his next opponent, challenging him for control of the bridge. Upon seeing her, Jax readied himself for battle, believing he had finally found a worthy opponent.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Fiora Laurent cuts a figure of both elegance and lethality. Her lithe frame is clad in gleaming armor, polished to a mirror shine. The armor itself is intricate Demacian steel, adorned with flourishes that hint at her noble heritage. Beneath the armor, glimpses of fine leather undergarments peek through, hinting at her agility. Her most striking feature is her face, framed by a cascade of fiery red hair. Her eyes, a piercing blue, radiate unwavering determination and focus. In her hand, she carries her signature weapon, a rapier named Riposte. The rapier's blade is as sharp and deadly as Fiora herself, its intricate filigree hinting at its legendary status.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Fiora is a swordswoman with a standoffish demeanor, direct with her words and frequently looking down on those who would seek to challenge her or her House. Since she was a child, her one true passion was studying the blade, and she strives for nothing less than absolute precision and excellence in her own technique. Having defeated numerous opponents, she claims she has no equal in the art of dueling, and has thus far refused all potential suitors. And though the other nobles simply dismissed her as an arrogant child, they have yet to prove her wrong in her conviction.

As a duelist, Fiora seeks honor for House Laurent, hoping to restore the family's reputation after numerous scandals and the death of her father Sebastien, where she was forced to slay him for his crimes. Fiora will avoid needless bloodshed if her opponent yields, but she is not averse to killing when she believes it is necessary. Whenever Fiora meets an opponent, or is insulted by a rival, she makes sure to challenge them before cutting them down with ruthless efficiency, but makes sure to give them the opportunity to back down and receive a lesser punishment instead. As a Demacian, Fiora is loyal to her kingdom and is willing to fight in its defense. She aims to elevate her House by making them useful to Demacia, dedicating her efforts to refinement of bladecraft and the training of the kingdom's swordsmen.

children.title: Jax
children.textContent: Jax might be the 'worthy opponent' Fiora longs for. He may also be interested in having her join his rebuilt Kohari.

children.title: House Crownguard
children.textContent: Fiora was set to marry a member of House Crownguard but rejected it, leading to her father Sebastien having to participate in a death duel. Tianna Crownguard, Garen and Lux's aunt, selected a veteran warrior from the Dauntless Vanguard to fight for her kinsman so that his defeat would be certain. This compelled Sebastien to cheat in the duel, dishonoring their House, and Fiora was forced to execute him so their family would not lose everything. Fiora and Tianna Crownguard remained on poor terms for several years after the scandal, but were eventually able to set aside their differences, with Tianna recognizing Fiora as an excellent, if somewhat brazen, swordswoman.

children.title: Garen Crownguard
children.textContent: Garen and Fiora know each other, as she helps train the Dauntless Vanguard, though they never train together as "the outcome would be obvious". They fought together against the dragon that attacked Ehrenmount.
